skill.name: stride-analysis-patterns
skill.description: Apply the STRIDE methodology to identify spoofing, tampering, repudiation, information disclosure, denial of service, and elevation of privilege threats.
skill.summary: Create structured STRIDE threat models for systems, components, and data flows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, ransomware extension, and hardcoded URL patterns. Review found the command and cryptography hits are false positives from Markdown fences, STRIDE terminology, and illustrative Python examples; the only retained indicator is non-executing reference links.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Detected as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are Markdown backtick fences and references to an example playbook, not executable Ruby or shell code. No evidence found of command execution by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are Markdown formatting or resource references. They do not invoke a shell, evaluate input, or define executable skill hooks.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Security Terminology Detected as Malicious Crypto
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and ransomware extension findings occur in threat-modeling terminology, dataclass fields, and examples that identify security risks. No evidence found of cryptographic implementation, ransomware behavior, or file modification.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are descriptive STRIDE content or Python examples for modeling threats. The skill contains no encryption routine, file-locking routine, or destructive file operation.

low_findings.title: Reference Links Require User-Initiated Network Access
low_findings.description: The network findings are three static documentation links to Microsoft, OWASP, and Wiley resources. They do not execute automatically, but they remain a minor external-link indicator for marketplace review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible references in a resources section. There is no fetch, curl, webhook, credential transfer, or automatic network call.

content.user_title: Analyze Systems with STRIDE Threat Modeling
content.value_statement: Security reviews often miss threats when teams use informal checklists. This skill applies STRIDE patterns to produce structured, repeatable threat analysis.
actual_capabilities: Maps system components to STRIDE threat categories.
Guides threat identification across trust boundaries and data flows.
Produces structured threat model sections and risk matrices.
Suggests common mitigations for each STRIDE category.
Provides templates for questionnaires, interaction analysis, and security documentation.
limitations: Does not replace expert security review or penetration testing.
Requires accurate system diagrams, assets, and trust boundary details.
Does not automatically inspect running systems or source repositories.
Mitigation suggestions must be validated against the real architecture.

use_cases.title: Review a New Architecture
use_cases.description: Identify likely threats before implementation and document controls for design approval.

use_cases.title: Prepare Sprint Security Work
use_cases.description: Convert design risks into prioritized mitigation tasks for engineering teams.

use_cases.title: Create Audit Evidence
use_cases.description: Produce a clear threat model summary for compliance, risk, or governance reviews.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic STRIDE Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner analysis for one application or feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use STRIDE to analyze this system. Ask me for missing assets, trust boundaries, data flows, and user roles before listing threats.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a Data Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Review a known flow between components.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply STRIDE to this data flow. Identify threats for each component, boundary crossing, and data store. Include impact, likelihood, and mitigations.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize Threats for Delivery
prompt_templates.scenario: Turn a threat list into engineering work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these STRIDE threats and rank them by risk. Group mitigations into immediate, short-term, and long-term engineering actions.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Complete Threat Model
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced documentation for review or audit.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete STRIDE threat model for this architecture. Include assets, trust boundaries, threat tables, risk scoring, mitigations, open assumptions, and review questions.

output_examples.input: Analyze an internet-facing web app that sends user profile data to an API and database.
output_examples.output: The output lists spoofing risks for authentication, tampering risks on API requests, information disclosure risks for profile data, and mitigations such as MFA, validation, encryption, and audit logging.

output_examples.input: Prioritize threats from a checkout service design review.
output_examples.output: The output ranks payment tampering, credential stuffing, and data exposure by impact and likelihood, then separates urgent controls from later hardening work.

output_examples.input: Create audit-ready notes for a service-to-service integration.
output_examples.output: The output summarizes assets, trust boundaries, applicable STRIDE categories, accepted assumptions, residual risks, and recommended owner actions.

best_practices: Provide current architecture diagrams, assets, actors, and trust boundaries.
Review every STRIDE category even when one seems unlikely.
Convert high-risk threats into owned mitigation tasks with deadlines.
anti_patterns: Do not treat template examples as proof that your system is secure.
Do not skip low-likelihood threats when impact is severe.
Do not publish a threat model without validating assumptions with system owners.

faq.question: What does STRIDE mean?
faq.answer: STRIDE covers spoofing, tampering, repudiation, information disclosure, denial of service, and elevation of privilege.

faq.question: Can this skill analyze source code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides structured threat modeling and needs system details supplied by the user.

faq.question: Is this useful without a data flow diagram?
faq.answer: Yes, but results improve when components, data stores, actors, and trust boundaries are clear.

faq.question: Does it provide mitigation guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It suggests common controls for each STRIDE category and helps prioritize them.

faq.question: Can it support compliance reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create structured notes that explain threats, controls, assumptions, and residual risks.

faq.question: Does it replace security testing?
faq.answer: No. Use it with design review, code review, penetration testing, and operational monitoring.
